sanskrit: दण्डो दमयतामस्मि नीतिरस्मि जिगीषताम् । मौनं चैवास्मि गुह्यानां ज्ञानं ज्ञानवतामहम् ॥
hindiTranslation: दमन करनेवालोंमें दण्डनीति और विजय चाहनेवालोंमें नीति मैं हूँ ।  गोपनीय भावोंमें मौन और ज्ञानवानोंमें ज्ञान मैं हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: Among enforcers, I am punishment; among those seeking victory, I am policy. Among secrets, I am silence; among the wise, I am knowledge.
essence: True power is measured by restraint, silence, and clear understanding.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says he appears as rightful force, strategic conduct, silence, and true understanding wherever they are found.
simpleInsight: Power does not always shout. The deepest strength can look like restraint, quiet, and clear understanding.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna stands frozen while Krishna reveals how the divine works through the world. Here Krishna names four forms of his presence: corrective force, wise strategy, silence, and the knowledge that belongs to the wise.
modernRelevance: You are in a room where one harsh word could break everything. The strongest move is not volume — it may be restraint, timing, and the calm choice to say only what is true.
translationLiteral: दण्डः (punishment/discipline) / दमयताम् (among those who restrain) / अस्मि (I am) / नीतिḥ (policy/right conduct) / अस्मि (I am) / जिगीषताम् (among those who seek victory) । / मौनम् (silence) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / अस्मि (I am) / गुह्यानाम् (among secrets) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / ज्ञानवताम् (among the wise) / अहम् (I am)
